meta.title: BMW 123d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 123d engine — N47 twin-turbo diesel. Hatch, coupé and convertible generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 123d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N47 twin-turbo engine typically costs £3,200–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

The N47 twin-turbo is rated ★★★☆☆. It shares the same rear-mounted timing chain design as the single-turbo N47, with the same cold-start rattle warning signs. The twin-turbo configuration adds complexity — turbocharger wear is an additional consideration. 2010+ cars with the N47TU revision have improved tensioners but are not immune.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 123d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear (rear-mounted, same as single-turbo N47), twin-turbocharger wear and boost issues, and EGR cooler coolant leaks. The twin-turbo setup adds complexity and potential turbo-related costs.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 123d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £5,000–£8,000 123d, a £4,300 recon is 54–86% of the car's value — assess carefully. For a cherished example, it's often worth it given the 123d's cult following.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 123d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 123d uses the N47S (pre-2010) or N47TU (2010+) twin-turbo diesel. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 123d? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing-chain history — the single most important check on any N47
* 2010+ build with the N47TU revision (improved tensioner)
* Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
avoidIf: * Any cold-start rattle with no chain history
* Loss of power or turbo noise without diagnosis
* Poor service history — the N47 twin-turbo is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 123d is a genuinely special car — a 200+ bhp diesel hatch with twin turbos and rear‑wheel drive. But the N47's rear-chain risk is real, and the twin‑turbo setup adds complexity. Buy on documented chain history, not on power figures alone.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 123d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning — critical on N47)
* Service History — Including which engine revision (pre-2010 N47S vs 2010+ N47TU)
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all N47 variants
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved boost or timing issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: * Turbocharger
items.value: Twin sequential turbo (Garrett GTB + variable geometry)

items.label: Most Common Failure
